_comment: Single source of truth for MCP servers across Claude Code and Codex. scripts/setup-mcp.sh reads this and registers each server in both tools (Claude: `claude mcp add-json --scope user`; Codex: appended [mcp_servers.*] in ~/.codex/config.toml). Secrets are referenced via ${ENV} expansion, never baked in. Servers with autoRegister:false are documented opt-ins (need a secret or a remote/OAuth flow) — see docs/04-mcp.md.

context7.description: Up-to-date, version-correct documentation and code examples for any library. Frontier: kills 'trained on an old API' hallucinations.
context7.note: Works without a key; set CONTEXT7_API_KEY for higher rate limits.

fetch.description: Fetch a URL and return clean, model-ready markdown. Gives Codex web-fetch parity with Claude's WebFetch.

git.description: Structured local git operations (status, diff, log, blame, show) as typed tools rather than parsed shell output.

github.codexSkipReason: Codex already provides GitHub via its github@openai-curated plugin — adding it here would duplicate.
github.description: GitHub issues/PRs/repos. Use the official remote server (OAuth) — recommended over the deprecated npm reference server.
github.setup: claude mcp add --transport http github https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/   # completes OAuth in-app

postgres.description: Read-only SQL over a Postgres database (schema introspection + SELECT). Best as a PROJECT server (e.g. project), gated on ${PROJECT_DATABASE_URL}.
postgres.note: Point at a read-replica or a read-only role. Never a write-capable prod credential.

browser.description: Drive a real browser (navigate, click, type, screenshot, extract) via Playwright. The sanctioned 'browser agent' — opt-in, for UI verification / scraping / repro of web bugs.
browser.setup: npx playwright install chromium   # one-time browser download, then enable in scripts/setup-mcp.sh
browser.note: Off by default — it can act on live sites. Use deliberately; scope to trusted pages. The agent that drives it still follows the same guardrails.

compass-memory.description: Cross-repo agent memory (record/search durable, non-secret learnings). Local SQLite, stdio. Trust tiers default to deny. See docs/adr/0001-cross-repo-memory.md.
compass-memory.setup: pip install 'mcp[cli]'; export COMPASS_MEMORY_TRUST='myrepo:read-write'; then register at project scope.
compass-memory.note: Opt-in, LOCAL v1 only (ADR 0001 Accepted for local; networked/team version is a separate gated decision). Secret-scrubbing is best-effort — never paste credentials. v1 reviewed by security-auditor: safe as local/stdio.
